speaker: POMPEY
text: Your hostages I have, so have you mine,

speaker: POMPEY
text: And we shall talk before we fight.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Most meet

speaker: CAESAR
text: That first we come to words, and therefore have we

speaker: CAESAR
text: Our written purposes before us sent,

speaker: CAESAR
text: Which if thou hast considered, let us know

speaker: CAESAR
text: If ’twill tie up thy discontented sword

speaker: CAESAR
text: And carry back to Sicily much tall youth

speaker: CAESAR
text: That else must perish here.

speaker: POMPEY
text: To you all three,

speaker: POMPEY
text: The senators alone of this great world,

speaker: POMPEY
text: Chief factors for the gods: I do not know

speaker: POMPEY
text: Wherefore my father should revengers want,

speaker: POMPEY
text: Having a son and friends, since Julius Caesar,

speaker: POMPEY
text: Who at Philippi the good Brutus ghosted,

speaker: POMPEY
text: There saw you laboring for him. What was ’t

speaker: POMPEY
text: That moved pale Cassius to conspire? And what

speaker: POMPEY
text: Made the all-honored, honest, Roman Brutus,

speaker: POMPEY
text: With the armed rest, courtiers of beauteous freedom,

speaker: POMPEY
text: To drench the Capitol, but that they would

speaker: POMPEY
text: Have one man but a man? And that is it

speaker: POMPEY
text: Hath made me rig my navy, at whose burden

speaker: POMPEY
text: The angered ocean foams, with which I meant

speaker: POMPEY
text: To scourge th’ ingratitude that despiteful Rome

speaker: POMPEY
text: Cast on my noble father.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Take your time.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Thou canst not fear us, Pompey, with thy sails.

speaker: ANTONY
text: We’ll speak with thee at sea. At land thou know’st

speaker: ANTONY
text: How much we do o’ercount thee.

speaker: POMPEY
text: At land indeed

speaker: POMPEY
text: Thou dost o’ercount me of my father’s house;

speaker: POMPEY
text: But since the cuckoo builds not for himself,

speaker: POMPEY
text: Remain in ’t as thou mayst.

speaker: LEPIDUS
text: Be pleased to tell us—

speaker: LEPIDUS
text: For this is from the present—how you take

speaker: LEPIDUS
text: The offers we have sent you.

speaker: CAESAR
text: There’s the point.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Which do not be entreated to, but weigh

speaker: ANTONY
text: What it is worth embraced.

speaker: CAESAR
text: And what may follow

speaker: CAESAR
text: To try a larger fortune.

speaker: POMPEY
text: You have made me offer

speaker: POMPEY
text: Of Sicily, Sardinia; and I must

speaker: POMPEY
text: Rid all the sea of pirates; then to send

speaker: POMPEY
text: Measures of wheat to Rome. This ’greed upon,

speaker: POMPEY
text: To part with unhacked edges and bear back

speaker: POMPEY
text: Our targes undinted.

speaker: ALL
text: That’s our offer.

speaker: POMPEY
text: Know then

speaker: POMPEY
text: I came before you here a man prepared

speaker: POMPEY
text: To take this offer. But Mark Antony

speaker: POMPEY
text: Put me to some impatience.—Though I lose

speaker: POMPEY
text: The praise of it by telling, you must know

speaker: POMPEY
text: When Caesar and your brother were at blows,

speaker: POMPEY
text: Your mother came to Sicily and did find

speaker: POMPEY
text: Her welcome friendly.

speaker: ANTONY
text: I have heard it, Pompey,

speaker: ANTONY
text: And am well studied for a liberal thanks,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Which I do owe you.

speaker: POMPEY
text: Let me have your hand.

speaker: POMPEY
text: I did not think, sir, to have met you here.

speaker: ANTONY
text: The beds i’ th’ East are soft; and thanks to you,

speaker: ANTONY
text: That called me timelier than my purpose hither,

speaker: ANTONY
text: For I have gained by ’t.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Since I saw you last,

speaker: CAESAR
text: There’s a change upon you.

speaker: POMPEY
text: Well, I know not

speaker: POMPEY
text: What counts harsh Fortune casts upon my face,

speaker: POMPEY
text: But in my bosom shall she never come

speaker: POMPEY
text: To make my heart her vassal.

speaker: LEPIDUS
text: Well met here.

speaker: POMPEY
text: I hope so, Lepidus. Thus we are agreed.

speaker: POMPEY
text: I crave our composition may be written

speaker: POMPEY
text: And sealed between us.

speaker: CAESAR
text: That’s the next to do.

speaker: POMPEY
text: We’ll feast each other ere we part, and let’s

speaker: POMPEY
text: Draw lots who shall begin.

speaker: ANTONY
text: That will I, Pompey.

speaker: POMPEY
text: No, Antony, take the lot. But, first or last,

speaker: POMPEY
text: Your fine Egyptian cookery shall have

speaker: POMPEY
text: The fame. I have heard that Julius Caesar

speaker: POMPEY
text: Grew fat with feasting there.

speaker: ANTONY
text: You have heard much.

speaker: POMPEY
text: I have fair meanings, sir.

speaker: ANTONY
text: And fair words to them.

speaker: POMPEY
text: Then so much have I heard.

speaker: POMPEY
text: And I have heard Apollodorus carried—

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: No more of that. He did so.

speaker: POMPEY
text: What, I pray you?

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: A certain queen to Caesar in a mattress.

speaker: POMPEY
text: I know thee now. How far’st thou, soldier?

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Well,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: And well am like to do, for I perceive

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Four feasts are toward.

speaker: POMPEY
text: Let me shake thy hand.

speaker: POMPEY
text: I never hated thee. I have seen thee fight

speaker: POMPEY
text: When I have envied thy behavior.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Sir,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: I never loved you much, but I ha’ praised you

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: When you have well deserved ten times as much

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: As I have said you did.

speaker: POMPEY
text: Enjoy thy plainness;

speaker: POMPEY
text: It nothing ill becomes thee.—

speaker: POMPEY
text: Aboard my galley I invite you all.

speaker: POMPEY
text: Will you lead, lords?

speaker: ALL
text: Show ’s the way, sir.

speaker: POMPEY
text: Come.

speaker: MENAS
text: Thy father, Pompey, would ne’er have

speaker: MENAS
text: made this treaty.—You and I have known, sir.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: At sea, I think.

speaker: MENAS
text: We have, sir.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: You have done well by water.

speaker: MENAS
text: And you by land.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: I will praise any man that will praise me,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: though it cannot be denied what I have done by

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: land.

speaker: MENAS
text: Nor what I have done by water.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Yes, something you can deny for your own

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: safety: you have been a great thief by sea.

speaker: MENAS
text: And you by land.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: There I deny my land service. But give me

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text:
your hand, Menas.
                 
                They
                 
                clasp
                 
                hands
                .
               If our eyes

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: had authority, here they might take two thieves

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: kissing.

speaker: MENAS
text: All men’s faces are true, whatsome’er their

speaker: MENAS
text: hands are.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: But there is never a fair woman has a true

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: face.

speaker: MENAS
text: No slander. They steal hearts.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: We came hither to fight with you.

speaker: MENAS
text: For my part, I am sorry it is turned to a

speaker: MENAS
text: drinking. Pompey doth this day laugh away his

speaker: MENAS
text: fortune.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: If he do, sure he cannot weep ’t back

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: again.

speaker: MENAS
text: You’ve said, sir. We looked not for Mark Antony

speaker: MENAS
text: here. Pray you, is he married to Cleopatra?

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Caesar’s sister is called Octavia.

speaker: MENAS
text: True, sir. She was the wife of Caius Marcellus.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: But she is now the wife of Marcus

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Antonius.

speaker: MENAS
text: Pray you, sir?

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: ’Tis true.

speaker: MENAS
text: Then is Caesar and he forever knit together.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: If I were bound to divine of this unity, I

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: would not prophesy so.

speaker: MENAS
text: I think the policy of that purpose made more in

speaker: MENAS
text: the marriage than the love of the parties.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: I think so, too. But you shall find the band

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: that seems to tie their friendship together will be

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: the very strangler of their amity. Octavia is of a holy,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: cold, and still conversation.

speaker: MENAS
text: Who would not have his wife so?

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Not he that himself is not so, which is

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Mark Antony. He will to his Egyptian dish again.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Then shall the sighs of Octavia blow the fire up in

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Caesar, and, as I said before, that which is the

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: strength of their amity shall prove the immediate

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: author of their variance. Antony will use his affection

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: where it is. He married but his occasion here.

speaker: MENAS
text: And thus it may be. Come, sir, will you aboard?

speaker: MENAS
text: I have a health for you.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: I shall take it, sir. We have used our throats

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: in Egypt.

speaker: MENAS
text: Come, let’s away.
